key: เตรทาเรเซีย
0.content: Tretaresia is an enormous fantasy world of imaginary power, where fantastical beasts and monsters roam and dungeons lie scattered like loose coins across the land. It is so vast that travel is measured in days, months, and even years, because each region lies a punishing distance from the next. A journey is never a straight line: keep to the roads and you pass villages, towns, waystations and caravans; stray off the path and you may stumble onto hidden dungeons, lost villages, strange cults and heretical faiths, or worse. There is no pure white anywhere in Tretaresia and no pure black either — the kind and the cruel are found mixed together in every land. The world is split into six far-flung continents, divided by great oceans torn open during the Great War a thousand years ago.

key: การเดินทาง
ออกเดินทาง
1.content: Distances in Tretaresia are immense; crossing a single continent can take weeks or months, and reaching another continent across the ocean can take a year or more. On the marked roads a traveler will meet villages, towns, waystations, merchant caravans, and fellow wanderers. Leave the road, however, and the world turns secret and strange — hidden dungeons, lost villages, reclusive cults, heretical religions, ruins, and dangers found nowhere on any map. What one encounters depends entirely on the land and the direction taken.

key: ทวีปเซ็นทรัล
ทวีปมนุษย์
2.content: The Central Continent, the so-called Human Continent, is the most ordinary of all six — and the most human, in every sense. For all its civility it is stained with the usual human filth: hatred, spite, greed, lust, and envy run beneath its courts and markets. It is the political and educational heart of the world, home to the Great Academy and, generally, to a single common currency shared across its kingdoms. Good people and wicked people live here side by side, as everywhere in Tretaresia.

key: ต้นไม้ยักษ์
3.content: The Great Forest is a colossal continent of forest and jungle. At its core grows an impossible tree, rising from the roots of the earth up past the clouds and beyond — so tall that no one knows how far it reaches, because no one has ever been there and returned. For the human race the place is taboo: any human who trespasses risks punishment, or the execution of their entire family. What lives beneath that endless canopy is largely unknown to the wider world.

key: ดินแดนไททัน
4.content: The Great Land of Titan is a continent of endless sand, whose peoples and kingdoms are far tougher and hardier than ordinary humans. Its greatest realm is the Grand Kingdom of Khaduzar, where a titanic pillar of stone shaped like a hand juts up out of the dunes — a hand clasping its own wrist, gripping itself. It is so monstrously large that distant kingdoms on the same continent can see it standing on the horizon. The desert hides oasis-cities, buried ruins, and dungeons beneath the sand.

key: ทวีปดรีโนเวีย
ดินแดนแห่งการต่อสู้
5.content: Drinovia is the land of battle, where most kingdoms prove themselves and settle their disputes through fights to the death. The weapons and remains of the fallen — swords, blades, whatever is left of the dead — are driven upright into the ground where they fell, planted like grave-markers across the whole of the earth. Strength is law here, and the landscape itself is a vast and silent graveyard of those who lost.

key: ดินแดนเหนือ
ดินแดนหิมะ
หิมะนิรันดร์
6.content: The North Continent is a winter land of constant, eternal snowfall, where the weather runs from cold to brutally, killingly cold — temperatures of -300° and lower, plunging deeper still depending on the year and the season. Survival itself is an achievement here, and only the hardiest peoples, beasts, and well-prepared travelers endure its white wastes.

key: บาลูกูเรีย
ทวีปบาลูกูเรีย
ดินแดนนักโทษ
7.content: Baluguria is a mid-sized continent that serves as the world's dumping ground for prisoners and slaves, who are shipped here as playthings and outlets for nobles and the rich. Gambling halls and night-trade pleasure houses are utterly ordinary; illegal goods and the criminal underworld make their home here. It is a place of vice, danger, and opportunity for those willing to deal in either.

key: รับรู้พลัง
สัมผัสพลัง
ตรวจจับมานา
8.content: As a universal rule in Tretaresia, every kind of power — mana included — can only be sensed by someone who wields the same kind of power. A False Magic user senses False Magic, a Blood Aura wielder senses Blood Aura, and so on. The sole exception is Formless Aura, which cannot be sensed by anyone at all. This means a person's true power can be completely invisible to those who do not share it.

key: เวทย์มนุษย์
ฟอลส์เมจิก
9.content: False Magic is 'human magic' — anyone can use it if they study and grasp its structure. Its output is high and casting is light on the body, but it must be channelled through a staff, wand, or other medium to amplify the energy into something powerful. It comes in many graded tiers, and is the common, well-understood magic of the present age.

key: เวทที่หายไป
10.content: True Magic is the true art, lost for over a thousand years. On the surface it looks almost identical to False Magic, near impossible to tell apart. It can be learned, but only by someone with a deep understanding of mana itself — and almost no one in the present age even knows it still exists. It is used exactly like human magic in every way, yet its effects are far more powerful, and it needs no medium at all when the wielder truly understands the power they are using.

key: พลังติดตัว
พลังกำเนิด
11.content: Aura is the in-born energy of this world — the local name for mana. Everyone is born with some, though how much varies wildly; some are born with none at all and live as perfectly ordinary people. Most who are born with Aura carry a single innate power from birth: an Origin skill they instinctively know how to use and understand its structure from the very start. Most such powers can be developed much further, but most people never realise this — they assume they already understand their own power completely, and so only ever use that one thing.

key: ออร่าไร้รูป
มานาไร้รูป
12.content: Formless Aura is a special form of mana, intangible and impossible to touch or sense — the one power in Tretaresia that no one can detect, by any means. Roughly one person is born with it every five hundred years, and it may fall at any point within that span (it might appear at year 30, 100, 400, or only at the full 500). Those born with Formless Aura almost always possess colossal power, on the scale of a world-class arch-demon.

key: ออร่าเลือด
มานาแวมไพร์
13.content: Blood Aura is the mana of vampires, drawing on power tied to blood — but used in astonishingly varied and creative ways, sometimes so cleverly disguised that onlookers never realise blood is involved at all. When a human is turned into a vampire, their ordinary Aura is transformed at once. Their original power may persist as-is, gain a new blood aspect, mutate into something wholly new, split into two powers (related or unrelated to the original) alongside a blood power — or it may be lost entirely. The outcome differs with every turning.

14.content: Sage Mana is 'immortal' or 'sage' power that can be trained — but no manual for it survives, and it is unknown in the present age. It is enormously potent, and mostly transformative in nature: from minor shapeshifting to changing one's entire body. It grants wildly varied abilities and, crucially, the power to draw on natural energy from the world to refill one's own mana, making its wielders dangerously self-sustaining.

key: ดิไวน์มานา
มานาบริสุทธิ์
มานาศักดิ์สิทธิ์
15.content: Divine Mana is the purest form of mana. It can access and switch freely between every other form of power as though changing modes at will, and it can neither be grasped nor sensed by ordinary means — only another wielder of Divine Mana can perceive it. It is among the rarest and most formidable powers in all of Tretaresia.

key: อาวุธพิเศษ
16.content: A Construct is a weapon or tool forged or crafted with a special Aura-like ability built into it, made so that those who have no Aura — or cannot reach the Aura they have — may cover their weakness with it. Their abilities are effectively endless, depending entirely on the individual piece that was made. A well-made Construct can let an ordinary person stand against the gifted.

key: ดิไวน์คอนสตรัค
อาวุธบรรพกาล
อาวุธเลือกเจ้าของ
17.content: Divine Constructs are Constructs that have existed since primordial times. Each can be wielded by one person alone — the weapon itself chooses its owner. They cannot be created anew, copied, imitated, or remade by any means whatsoever; their structure is far too complex for human understanding. To bear one is to hold a relic of the ancient world that answers to no one else.

key: เผ่าพันธุ์
18.content: The races of Tretaresia are many and all but countless. Humans, demons, beastfolk, vampires, monstrous peoples, and far stranger things all share the world. Some races are seen everywhere and commonly; some are never seen at all unless deliberately sought out and found; and some are vanishingly, legendarily rare. Each carries its own culture, lifespan, and relationship to power.

19.content: Monsters roam across Tretaresia, from lowly pests to world-shaking beasts. Most of them — Orcs, Trolls, Goblins, and the like — can speak human language well enough to be understood, but their grammar is badly broken: intelligible, yet strange, halting, and full of errors. One can hold a conversation with such a creature, though it will never sound quite right.

key: สงครามใหญ่
ปีศาจบรรพกาล
20.comment: History — The Great War (1000 years ago)
20.content: A thousand years ago the Great War between demons and humans erupted and broke the world apart, splitting the land into pieces and tearing open vast new oceans across many regions. It ended with the death of the leaders of both sides: the hero Ars was slain, while the Primordial Demon — the demon side's ancient leader — was sealed away in a dimension where time never flows, imprisoned there for all eternity. The scars of that war shaped the six continents as they exist today.

key: สร้างอารยธรรม
หลังสงคราม
21.content: Some four hundred years after the Great War, humans and the civilisations of the many races carved out their own territories and built civilisation anew. They lived in an uneasy harmony that respected one another's lands — even as internal wars, invasions, raids, and the rise of the slave trade festered beneath that surface peace. Much of the present world order traces its roots to this rebuilding age.

key: ยุคปัจจุบัน
22.comment: History — The Present Age (600 years after)
22.content: Roughly six hundred years after the rebuilding lies the present day: a world that holds both light and shadow at once, in every land at the same time. There is no such thing as purely white here — and yet that does not mean good people are absent. The good and the wicked are encountered as a matter of course, accumulated and mingled together throughout every corner of Tretaresia.

key: มหาวิทยาลัย
23.content: The Great Academy, the world's foremost institution of learning, was founded in the human lands. Tuition is steep, and entry takes a great deal of money — but as a matter of policy it admits every race without distinction, all are welcome through its gates. Even so, contempt, snobbery, and old prejudice still linger within its halls, no matter the official creed of equality.

key: กิลด์นักผจญภัย
24.content: Adventurer's Guilds are established everywhere humans live, and reach even into demon lands — dangerous places, but ones that nonetheless welcome human adventurers, give or take the occasional mockery or scorn from certain groups. The Guild offers work of every kind, from monster-slaying to escort and exploration, and ranks its members by proven ability. Professions in Tretaresia are many and open to all; adventuring is only one of countless paths.

key: ระดับนักผจญภัย
25.content: Adventurers are graded by rank, from lowest to highest: ROOKIE — the starting rank, or the rank of those judged weak or unimpressive by the assessor at registration. BASIC — for those who have begun to show real competence. INTERMEDIATE — a high rank trusted with greater missions and widely respected; most adventurers earn their personal title at this rank. EMBER — higher still, acknowledged in skill and reasonably famous, able to be headhunted or to set their own terms. CUSTOM RANK — a rank whose very name the assessor personally invents for the individual; few ever reach it, and those who do are absurdly strong, absurdly cool, living legends made flesh.

26.content: Dungeons exist in staggering numbers across Tretaresia, found both along the roads and hidden far off them. Each is graded by rank according to its danger, from weakest to strongest: E-, E, E+, D-, D, D+, C-, C, C+, B-, B, B+, A-, A, A+, S-, S, S+, and finally SS. A dungeon's rank warns adventurers what horrors and rewards may wait inside; the highest ranks are death sentences for all but the legendary.

27.content: Currency in Tretaresia varies by region. Different lands — especially the non-human territories — use different kinds of money entirely; what is accepted depends on exactly where one happens to be. The Central (human) Continent generally shares a single common currency across its kingdoms, which makes trade there far simpler than in the patchwork of coinages found elsewhere in the world.

28.content: Slavery and the underworld are realities of Tretaresia, however ugly. Prisoners and slaves are bought, sold, and shipped — most infamously to Baluguria, where they become playthings and outlets for nobles and the rich. Gambling, illegal goods, and criminal undergrounds thrive in the shadows of every continent, even as respectable society pretends not to see them. It is part of the world's pervasive grey: no land is wholly clean.

key: รสนิยมทางเพศ

แท็กแฟนตาซี

### USAGE & LIMITS (BINDING)

30.content:
## STATUS SYSTEM — {{user}}'s LIVING SHEET

At the VERY END (bottom) of EVERY response, after all narration, append a status block shaped exactly like this:

<TR_STATUS>
{ ...JSON... }
</TR_STATUS>

It renders (via regex) into a five-tab widget — Profile, Skills, Party, Quests, Bonds. You receive the raw JSON; the reader sees only the widget. NEVER print, narrate, or describe the raw JSON or the tags. Carry ui_theme and ui_lang forward from the character's creation/confirm sheet. Update values every turn as the story changes them.

### JSON SCHEMA (include every key you have data for; omit or blank the ones you don't)
{
  "name":"", "title":"", "race":"", "age":"", "gender":"",
  "rank":"", "guild":"", "occupation":"", "reputation":"", "location":"",
  "power_types":[],
  "currency":{ "gold":0, "silver":0, "copper":0, "green":0, "iron":0, "stone":0, "note":"" },
  "vitals":{ "hp":{"cur":100,"max":100}, "stamina":{"cur":100,"max":100}, "aura":{"cur":100,"max":100} },
  "attributes":[ {"name":"Swordsmanship","level":5,"desc":""} ],
  "skills":[ {"name":"","type":"","tier":"","desc":""} ],
  "party":[ {"name":"","race":"","role":"","rank":"","status":"","note":""} ],
  "quests":[ {"name":"","rank":"","info":"","objective":"","status":"ongoing","reward":""} ],
  "relationships":{
     "user":{ "arousal":0, "corruption":0, "kinks":[] },
     "partners":[ {"name":"","relation":"","affection":0,"trust":0,"desire":0,"corruption":0,"infidelity":0,"kinks":[],"note":""} ]
  },
  "ui_theme":"dark", "ui_lang":"en"
}

### TAB / FIELD NOTES
- PROFILE tab: name, title, race, age, gender, rank, guild, occupation, reputation, location; power_types (array of the character's power systems — render as chips); vitals as bars; currency wallet. Only nonzero coins display. Currency differs by region (see the "Currency & Trade" entry) — the Central Continent uses a shared coinage; other lands may use green/iron/stone or local money. Optional currency.note.
- SKILLS tab: `attributes` is a list of skills/stats each rated 0-10 that GROW with practice (include BOTH adventuring skills — Swordsmanship, Magic Control, Stealth, Detection, Survival, Negotiation, Leadership — AND everyday ones like Cooking, Riding, Lockpicking). `skills` is the Skill Storage: named powers/techniques with type (e.g. Origin, Blood, Movement) and tier (Rookie/Novice/Adept/Expert/Master or your own).
- PARTY tab: `party` lists current companions with race, role, rank, status (health/condition), and a short note. Empty = travelling alone.
- QUESTS tab: `quests` lists contracts/objectives with rank (E- … SS for dungeons, or guild rank), info, objective, status ("ongoing" / "done" / "failed"), and reward. Keep completed and failed ones briefly for record.
- BONDS tab (Relationships, 18+): `relationships.user` tracks the protagonist's own arousal and corruption (0-100) and any kinks/tags that have surfaced. `relationships.partners` tracks each significant bond with meters 0-100 (affection, trust, desire, corruption, infidelity), a relation label, shared kinks, and a note.

### BINDING RULES
- All meters are 0-100; attribute levels are 0-10. Keep them consistent turn to turn; nudge them by what actually happened, don't reset them.
- Record a kink/tag on a Bond ONLY after it genuinely surfaces, is requested, or is confessed in play (draw labels from the "Kink, Fetish & Tag Library" entry). Never pre-assign or invent {{user}}'s kinks. {{user}}'s real-time limits and any safeword/stop always override.
- Detection truth: powers are sensed only by those sharing the same kind (Formless by none). An NPC's sheet/knowledge should reflect what they could actually perceive.
- Output the block silently as the final element of every reply; never mention it in prose.

entries.content: The Great Forest is a colossal continent of forest and jungle. At its core grows an impossible tree, rising from the roots of the earth up past the clouds and beyond — so tall that no one knows how far it reaches, because no one has ever been there and returned. For the human race the place is taboo: any human who trespasses risks punishment, or the execution of their entire family. What lives beneath that endless canopy is largely unknown to the wider world.

keys: ดินแดนไททัน
entries.content: The Great Land of Titan is a continent of endless sand, whose peoples and kingdoms are far tougher and hardier than ordinary humans. Its greatest realm is the Grand Kingdom of Khaduzar, where a titanic pillar of stone shaped like a hand juts up out of the dunes — a hand clasping its own wrist, gripping itself. It is so monstrously large that distant kingdoms on the same continent can see it standing on the horizon. The desert hides oasis-cities, buried ruins, and dungeons beneath the sand.

keys: ทวีปดรีโนเวีย
ดินแดนแห่งการต่อสู้
entries.content: Drinovia is the land of battle, where most kingdoms prove themselves and settle their disputes through fights to the death. The weapons and remains of the fallen — swords, blades, whatever is left of the dead — are driven upright into the ground where they fell, planted like grave-markers across the whole of the earth. Strength is law here, and the landscape itself is a vast and silent graveyard of those who lost.

keys: ดินแดนเหนือ
ดินแดนหิมะ
หิมะนิรันดร์
entries.content: The North Continent is a winter land of constant, eternal snowfall, where the weather runs from cold to brutally, killingly cold — temperatures of -300° and lower, plunging deeper still depending on the year and the season. Survival itself is an achievement here, and only the hardiest peoples, beasts, and well-prepared travelers endure its white wastes.

keys: บาลูกูเรีย
ทวีปบาลูกูเรีย
ดินแดนนักโทษ
entries.content: Baluguria is a mid-sized continent that serves as the world's dumping ground for prisoners and slaves, who are shipped here as playthings and outlets for nobles and the rich. Gambling halls and night-trade pleasure houses are utterly ordinary; illegal goods and the criminal underworld make their home here. It is a place of vice, danger, and opportunity for those willing to deal in either.

keys: รับรู้พลัง
สัมผัสพลัง
ตรวจจับมานา
entries.content: As a universal rule in Tretaresia, every kind of power — mana included — can only be sensed by someone who wields the same kind of power. A False Magic user senses False Magic, a Blood Aura wielder senses Blood Aura, and so on. The sole exception is Formless Aura, which cannot be sensed by anyone at all. This means a person's true power can be completely invisible to those who do not share it.

keys: เวทย์มนุษย์
ฟอลส์เมจิก
entries.content: False Magic is 'human magic' — anyone can use it if they study and grasp its structure. Its output is high and casting is light on the body, but it must be channelled through a staff, wand, or other medium to amplify the energy into something powerful. It comes in many graded tiers, and is the common, well-understood magic of the present age.

keys: เวทที่หายไป
entries.content: True Magic is the true art, lost for over a thousand years. On the surface it looks almost identical to False Magic, near impossible to tell apart. It can be learned, but only by someone with a deep understanding of mana itself — and almost no one in the present age even knows it still exists. It is used exactly like human magic in every way, yet its effects are far more powerful, and it needs no medium at all when the wielder truly understands the power they are using.

keys: พลังติดตัว
พลังกำเนิด
entries.content: Aura is the in-born energy of this world — the local name for mana. Everyone is born with some, though how much varies wildly; some are born with none at all and live as perfectly ordinary people. Most who are born with Aura carry a single innate power from birth: an Origin skill they instinctively know how to use and understand its structure from the very start. Most such powers can be developed much further, but most people never realise this — they assume they already understand their own power completely, and so only ever use that one thing.

keys: ออร่าไร้รูป
มานาไร้รูป
entries.content: Formless Aura is a special form of mana, intangible and impossible to touch or sense — the one power in Tretaresia that no one can detect, by any means. Roughly one person is born with it every five hundred years, and it may fall at any point within that span (it might appear at year 30, 100, 400, or only at the full 500). Those born with Formless Aura almost always possess colossal power, on the scale of a world-class arch-demon.

keys: ออร่าเลือด
มานาแวมไพร์
entries.content: Blood Aura is the mana of vampires, drawing on power tied to blood — but used in astonishingly varied and creative ways, sometimes so cleverly disguised that onlookers never realise blood is involved at all. When a human is turned into a vampire, their ordinary Aura is transformed at once. Their original power may persist as-is, gain a new blood aspect, mutate into something wholly new, split into two powers (related or unrelated to the original) alongside a blood power — or it may be lost entirely. The outcome differs with every turning.

Sage Mana is 'immortal' or 'sage' power that can be trained — but no manual for it survives, and it is unknown in the present age. It is enormously potent, and mostly transformative in nature: from minor shapeshifting to changing one's entire body. It grants wildly varied abilities and, crucially, the power to draw on natural energy from the world to refill one's own mana, making its wielders dangerously self-sustaining.

keys: ดิไวน์มานา
มานาบริสุทธิ์
มานาศักดิ์สิทธิ์
entries.content: Divine Mana is the purest form of mana. It can access and switch freely between every other form of power as though changing modes at will, and it can neither be grasped nor sensed by ordinary means — only another wielder of Divine Mana can perceive it. It is among the rarest and most formidable powers in all of Tretaresia.

keys: อาวุธพิเศษ
entries.content: A Construct is a weapon or tool forged or crafted with a special Aura-like ability built into it, made so that those who have no Aura — or cannot reach the Aura they have — may cover their weakness with it. Their abilities are effectively endless, depending entirely on the individual piece that was made. A well-made Construct can let an ordinary person stand against the gifted.

keys: ดิไวน์คอนสตรัค
อาวุธบรรพกาล
อาวุธเลือกเจ้าของ
entries.content: Divine Constructs are Constructs that have existed since primordial times. Each can be wielded by one person alone — the weapon itself chooses its owner. They cannot be created anew, copied, imitated, or remade by any means whatsoever; their structure is far too complex for human understanding. To bear one is to hold a relic of the ancient world that answers to no one else.

keys: เผ่าพันธุ์
entries.content: The races of Tretaresia are many and all but countless. Humans, demons, beastfolk, vampires, monstrous peoples, and far stranger things all share the world. Some races are seen everywhere and commonly; some are never seen at all unless deliberately sought out and found; and some are vanishingly, legendarily rare. Each carries its own culture, lifespan, and relationship to power.

Monsters roam across Tretaresia, from lowly pests to world-shaking beasts. Most of them — Orcs, Trolls, Goblins, and the like — can speak human language well enough to be understood, but their grammar is badly broken: intelligible, yet strange, halting, and full of errors. One can hold a conversation with such a creature, though it will never sound quite right.

keys: สงครามใหญ่
ปีศาจบรรพกาล
entries.comment: History — The Great War (1000 years ago)
entries.content: A thousand years ago the Great War between demons and humans erupted and broke the world apart, splitting the land into pieces and tearing open vast new oceans across many regions. It ended with the death of the leaders of both sides: the hero Ars was slain, while the Primordial Demon — the demon side's ancient leader — was sealed away in a dimension where time never flows, imprisoned there for all eternity. The scars of that war shaped the six continents as they exist today.

keys: สร้างอารยธรรม
หลังสงคราม
entries.content: Some four hundred years after the Great War, humans and the civilisations of the many races carved out their own territories and built civilisation anew. They lived in an uneasy harmony that respected one another's lands — even as internal wars, invasions, raids, and the rise of the slave trade festered beneath that surface peace. Much of the present world order traces its roots to this rebuilding age.

keys: ยุคปัจจุบัน
entries.comment: History — The Present Age (600 years after)
entries.content: Roughly six hundred years after the rebuilding lies the present day: a world that holds both light and shadow at once, in every land at the same time. There is no such thing as purely white here — and yet that does not mean good people are absent. The good and the wicked are encountered as a matter of course, accumulated and mingled together throughout every corner of Tretaresia.

keys: มหาวิทยาลัย
entries.content: The Great Academy, the world's foremost institution of learning, was founded in the human lands. Tuition is steep, and entry takes a great deal of money — but as a matter of policy it admits every race without distinction, all are welcome through its gates. Even so, contempt, snobbery, and old prejudice still linger within its halls, no matter the official creed of equality.

keys: กิลด์นักผจญภัย
entries.content: Adventurer's Guilds are established everywhere humans live, and reach even into demon lands — dangerous places, but ones that nonetheless welcome human adventurers, give or take the occasional mockery or scorn from certain groups. The Guild offers work of every kind, from monster-slaying to escort and exploration, and ranks its members by proven ability. Professions in Tretaresia are many and open to all; adventuring is only one of countless paths.

keys: ระดับนักผจญภัย
entries.content: Adventurers are graded by rank, from lowest to highest: ROOKIE — the starting rank, or the rank of those judged weak or unimpressive by the assessor at registration. BASIC — for those who have begun to show real competence. INTERMEDIATE — a high rank trusted with greater missions and widely respected; most adventurers earn their personal title at this rank. EMBER — higher still, acknowledged in skill and reasonably famous, able to be headhunted or to set their own terms. CUSTOM RANK — a rank whose very name the assessor personally invents for the individual; few ever reach it, and those who do are absurdly strong, absurdly cool, living legends made flesh.

Dungeons exist in staggering numbers across Tretaresia, found both along the roads and hidden far off them. Each is graded by rank according to its danger, from weakest to strongest: E-, E, E+, D-, D, D+, C-, C, C+, B-, B, B+, A-, A, A+, S-, S, S+, and finally SS. A dungeon's rank warns adventurers what horrors and rewards may wait inside; the highest ranks are death sentences for all but the legendary.

Currency in Tretaresia varies by region. Different lands — especially the non-human territories — use different kinds of money entirely; what is accepted depends on exactly where one happens to be. The Central (human) Continent generally shares a single common currency across its kingdoms, which makes trade there far simpler than in the patchwork of coinages found elsewhere in the world.

Slavery and the underworld are realities of Tretaresia, however ugly. Prisoners and slaves are bought, sold, and shipped — most infamously to Baluguria, where they become playthings and outlets for nobles and the rich. Gambling, illegal goods, and criminal undergrounds thrive in the shadows of every continent, even as respectable society pretends not to see them. It is part of the world's pervasive grey: no land is wholly clean.

entries.content:
## KINK, FETISH & TAG LIBRARY (18+ REFERENCE)

A vocabulary reference for the Relationships tab of the Status System and for content tagging. Use these labels to record a kink, fetish, or theme that has ACTUALLY surfaced for {{user}} or a partner in play. All characters are adults (18+); this is fictional, consensual adult roleplay between adults only. Pick a short tag from below (or a close equivalent) and render it in {{user}}'s display language while keeping the meaning recognizable. Ordering runs roughly vanilla -> extreme. ### TIER 20 — Hardcore / Degenerate (extreme, fiction only, all adults)

### TIER 21 — TRETARESIA Setting-Specific (fantasy)
Blood Aura feeding/blood-bond (vampire), Fang-marking & turning (consensual), Beastfolk heat & scent (adult), Sage-Mana shapeshifting tryst, Aura-resonance (only same-power partners feel it), Formless-Aura overwhelm (fiction), Divine-Construct possessive bond ("the weapon chose you"), Mana-exhaustion afterglow, Dungeon-delve adrenaline tryst, Adventurer-party bond, Slave-crest obedience (Baluguria seals), Pleasure-house contract (Baluguria, adult), Aphrodisiac-alchemy, Healing-magic regeneration kink, Monster-tongue/Orc broken-speech pairing (adult), Titan-folk size-gap pairing, North-Continent shared-warmth survival, Cursed-relic corruption, Academy rivals (adult), Rank/power-gap pairing.

### USAGE & LIMITS (BINDING)
- Record a kink on the Relationships tab ONLY after it genuinely appears, is requested, or is confessed in play. Never pre-assign or invent {{user}}'s kinks.
- Tier 20 (Hardcore/Degenerate) and the most extreme Tier 17 tags are DARK-THEME, fiction-only devices. Surface them ONLY when {{user}} has clearly opted in; never spring them unprompted.
- Cross-Race/Status-play (Tier 19) is fictional fantasy between consenting adult characters only — never real-world hate or harassment.
- CNC, dubcon and similar are consensual-roleplay devices. {{user}}'s real-time refusals, limits, and any safeword/stop are ALWAYS binding and override any tag.

## STATUS SYSTEM — {{user}}'s LIVING SHEET

At the VERY END (bottom) of EVERY response, after all narration, append a status block shaped exactly like this:

<TR_STATUS>
{ ...JSON... }
</TR_STATUS>

It renders (via regex) into a five-tab widget — Profile, Skills, Party, Quests, Bonds. You receive the raw JSON; the reader sees only the widget. NEVER print, narrate, or describe the raw JSON or the tags. Carry ui_theme and ui_lang forward from the character's creation/confirm sheet. Update values every turn as the story changes them.

### JSON SCHEMA (include every key you have data for; omit or blank the ones you don't)
{
  "name":"", "title":"", "race":"", "age":"", "gender":"",
  "rank":"", "guild":"", "occupation":"", "reputation":"", "location":"",
  "power_types":[],
  "currency":{ "gold":0, "silver":0, "copper":0, "green":0, "iron":0, "stone":0, "note":"" },
  "vitals":{ "hp":{"cur":100,"max":100}, "stamina":{"cur":100,"max":100}, "aura":{"cur":100,"max":100} },
  "attributes":[ {"name":"Swordsmanship","level":5,"desc":""} ],
  "skills":[ {"name":"","type":"","tier":"","desc":""} ],
  "party":[ {"name":"","race":"","role":"","rank":"","status":"","note":""} ],
  "quests":[ {"name":"","rank":"","info":"","objective":"","status":"ongoing","reward":""} ],
  "relationships":{
     "user":{ "arousal":0, "corruption":0, "kinks":[] },
     "partners":[ {"name":"","relation":"","affection":0,"trust":0,"desire":0,"corruption":0,"infidelity":0,"kinks":[],"note":""} ]
  },
  "ui_theme":"dark", "ui_lang":"en"
}

### TAB / FIELD NOTES
- PROFILE tab: name, title, race, age, gender, rank, guild, occupation, reputation, location; power_types (array of the character's power systems — render as chips); vitals as bars; currency wallet. Only nonzero coins display. Currency differs by region (see the "Currency & Trade" entry) — the Central Continent uses a shared coinage; other lands may use green/iron/stone or local money. Optional currency.note.
- SKILLS tab: `attributes` is a list of skills/stats each rated 0-10 that GROW with practice (include BOTH adventuring skills — Swordsmanship, Magic Control, Stealth, Detection, Survival, Negotiation, Leadership — AND everyday ones like Cooking, Riding, Lockpicking). `skills` is the Skill Storage: named powers/techniques with type (e.g. Origin, Blood, Movement) and tier (Rookie/Novice/Adept/Expert/Master or your own).
- PARTY tab: `party` lists current companions with race, role, rank, status (health/condition), and a short note. Empty = travelling alone.
- QUESTS tab: `quests` lists contracts/objectives with rank (E- … SS for dungeons, or guild rank), info, objective, status ("ongoing" / "done" / "failed"), and reward. Keep completed and failed ones briefly for record.
- BONDS tab (Relationships, 18+): `relationships.user` tracks the protagonist's own arousal and corruption (0-100) and any kinks/tags that have surfaced. `relationships.partners` tracks each significant bond with meters 0-100 (affection, trust, desire, corruption, infidelity), a relation label, shared kinks, and a note.

### BINDING RULES
- All meters are 0-100; attribute levels are 0-10. Keep them consistent turn to turn; nudge them by what actually happened, don't reset them.
- Record a kink/tag on a Bond ONLY after it genuinely surfaces, is requested, or is confessed in play (draw labels from the "Kink, Fetish & Tag Library" entry). Never pre-assign or invent {{user}}'s kinks. {{user}}'s real-time limits and any safeword/stop always override.
- Detection truth: powers are sensed only by those sharing the same kind (Formless by none). An NPC's sheet/knowledge should reflect what they could actually perceive.
- Output the block silently as the final element of every reply; never mention it in prose.
